article:
In brief: today will be another hot and humid day in Abuja, with temperatures feeling like 31°C despite the actual temperature being 27°C. We're expecting a mostly dry week, with the exception of a potentially wet weekend, particularly on Saturday when there's a 68% chance of rain. If you're planning on visiting the National Assembly or the Nigerian National Mosque, make sure to stay hydrated and wear light, breathable clothing.

As we take a look at the current conditions, it's clear that the heat is already making its presence known. With a feels-like temperature of 31°C, it's essential to take precautions to avoid heat exhaustion, especially if you're planning on spending time outdoors. The humidity is also relatively high at 80%, which can make the heat feel even more oppressive. On the bright side, the wind is blowing at a gentle 6 km/h, which might provide some slight relief.

If you're planning on traveling around the city today, you should be able to get around without too much trouble. The Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently experiencing light traffic, with speeds of 38 km/h, which is on par with free-flow traffic. However, it's always a good idea to check for updates before heading out, especially during peak hours. And don't forget to fill up on gas and check your vehicle's tire pressure, as the heat can cause tires to degrade faster.

As we head into the rest of the week, it's looking like we can expect more of the same hot and dry conditions. [MAP: temperature forecast for the next 7 days] shows that temperatures will remain in the mid-30s, with highs reaching as much as 37°C on Monday and Tuesday. While it's not uncommon for Abuja to experience high temperatures during this time of year, it's still essential to take precautions to stay safe. Make sure to drink plenty of water, wear light and loose-fitting clothing, and take breaks in cool, shaded areas.

### Thursday, April 17
Today will be a relatively dry day, with only an 18% chance of rain. Temperatures will soar to a high of 35°C, while the low will dip to 26°C. If you're planning on attending an event at the Abuja Stadium, you should be able to enjoy the festivities without worrying about the weather. However, do make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade if needed.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will gradually decrease, but it will still feel relatively warm. If you're planning on enjoying a night out in the city, you should be able to get around without too much trouble. Just be aware that the heat can sometimes cause power outages, so it's a good idea to have a backup plan in case the lights go out.

### Friday, April 18
On Friday, we're expecting a slight increase in the chance of rain, with a 33% chance of precipitation. Temperatures will remain relatively consistent, with a high of 34°C and a low of 26°C. If you're planning on visiting the Jabi Lake, you should be able to enjoy the scenery without worrying about the weather. However, do keep an eye on the skies, as a sudden downpour could occur.

As we head into the weekend, it's looking like we can expect some potentially wet weather. While it's not a guarantee, the models are suggesting that we could see some significant rainfall on Saturday. If you're planning on traveling, you should be aware that the roads could become slippery and treacherous, especially on the Kubwa-Abuja Expressway. Make sure to check the weather forecast before heading out and consider alternative routes if possible.

### Saturday, April 19
On Saturday, we're expecting a significant chance of rain, with a 68% chance of precipitation. Temperatures will drop slightly, with a high of 30°C and a low of 25°C. If you're planning on attending an outdoor event, you should be prepared for the possibility of rain. [MAP: precipitation forecast for Saturday] shows that the rain could be quite widespread, so it's essential to have a backup plan in place.

As we head into the evening, the rain should begin to clear, leaving behind a cooler and more pleasant atmosphere. If you're planning on enjoying a night out in the city, you should be able to get around without too much trouble. Just be aware that the roads could still be slippery, so it's essential to exercise caution when driving.

### Sunday, April 20
On Sunday, we're expecting a return to dry conditions, with only a 39% chance of rain. Temperatures will soar to a high of 37°C, while the low will dip to 25°C. If you're planning on visiting the Aso Rock, you should be able to enjoy the views without worrying about the weather. However, do make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade if needed.

As we head into the rest of the week, it's looking like we can expect more of the same hot and dry conditions. While it's not uncommon for Abuja to experience high temperatures during this time of year, it's still essential to take precautions to stay safe. Make sure to drink plenty of water, wear light and loose-fitting clothing, and take breaks in cool, shaded areas. And don't forget to check the weather forecast regularly, as conditions can change quickly in our city.
